creator: thierry@FreeBSD.org
text:
According to Mikhail Teterin <mi+kde@aldan.algebra.com> on Mon 26 mar =20
 07:37:10 2007 :
 
 > On Monday 26 March 2007 01:26, Thierry Thomas wrote:
 > =3D The tests are not failing, and I don't propose to disable them, but ju=
 st
 > =3D to move them to the regression-test target (which is automatically
 > =3D launched by pointyhat or tinderbox).
 >
 > The error log:
 >
 > http://pointyhat.freebsd.org/errorlogs/amd64-errorlogs/e.5.2007032316/tclX=
 -8.4.log
 >
 > shows some failures in the nice.test, which are fatal in the current versi=
 on
 > of the port.
 
 The failure in the tests is not fatal on my machine.
 
 > =3D The failure is when the port rebuilds man pages, and it is addressed b=
 y
 > =3D patch-af.
 >
 > Khmm, this I was not aware off. Nor do I see it in my builds. Your patch m=
 ay,
 > indeed, fix that, but I'd like to figure out, what's wrong with the nice
 > command now.
 >
 > It was all working fine for months (years?) until something changed in the
 > jail-implementation...
 
 But this failure is fatal for me, and I'm the culprit... Since =20
 revision v 1.91 of ports/lang/tcl84/Makefile, the macros included in =20
 the man pages of TCL exist, and was not handled correctly.
 
 Regards,
 --=20
 Th. Thomas.
